title: Holding Child Predators Accountable Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill is about punishing people who commit crimes with illegal pictures and materials that hurt children. Right now, these crimes have different punishments depending on what someone did. The new bill would change this so that anyone found guilty of these crimes would get either the death penalty or life in prison. They would also have to pay money as a fine. The goal is to make the punishments much more serious for people who harm children this way.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Holding Child Predators Accountable Act.

sections.title: Section 2. Possession of Child Porn
sections.text: This section changes the punishments for three different types of crimes involving children. First, it changes the punishment for obscenity crimes. Under current law in section 1466A of the United States criminal code, people who commit these crimes get certain penalties. This bill changes those penalties so that anyone who commits these crimes will be fined and either sentenced to death or sent to prison for life. Second, it changes the punishment for exploitation material crimes. Under current law in section 2252 of the United States criminal code, the punishment varies depending on the specific crime. This bill changes that so anyone who violates any of the four main parts of this law, or tries to commit these crimes, or plans with others to commit these crimes, will be fined and either sentenced to death or sent to prison for life. Third, it changes the punishment for pornography material crimes. Under current law in section 2252A of the United States criminal code, the punishment varies depending on the specific crime. This bill changes that so anyone who violates any of the seven main parts of this law, or tries to commit these crimes, or plans with others to commit these crimes, will be fined and either sentenced to death or sent to prison for life.
